title: The finite DC mode is additive

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The DC sum of the pointwise sum of two finite signals equals the sum of their DC sums.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
u and v are real-valued signals on s
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite DC functional is additive.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteDC_add
declarations.statement: theorem finiteDC_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) : finiteDC s (fun i => u i + v i) = finiteDC s u + finiteDC s v

tasks.input: Translate additivity of the finite DC mode into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteDC_add {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u v : ι → Real) : finiteDC s (fun i => u i + v i) = finiteDC s u + finiteDC s v

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding finiteDC and applying finite-sum additivity.
Find the finite-sum additivity lemma needed for this proof.
